name: Van Alexander
abstract: Van Alexander (May 2, 1915 – July 19, 2015) was an American bandleader, arranger, and composer.

about_quotes.speaker: Mandel Johnny
about_quotes.evidence: the very first teacher I went to was Van Alexander... And the most valuable thing I ever learned, I learned from Van Alexander in my first lesson
about_quotes.doc_title: Mandel Johnny — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Gerald Wilson
about_quotes.evidence: You know Van Alexander? [INTERVIEWER] Oh yeah, we interviewed him last year. >> [INTERVIEWEE] Oh yeah, Van.
about_quotes.doc_title: Gerald Wilson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Plas Johnson
about_quotes.evidence: one of the first arrangers to use me in that capacity... was Les Baxter and maybe Van Alexander
about_quotes.doc_title: Plas Johnson interviewed by Monk Rowe, Sarasota, Florida, April 12, 1996

about_quotes.speaker: Jonah Jones
about_quotes.evidence: Van Alexander's going to make the arrangements... So we did, we went down and made the record

about_quotes.speaker: Teddy McRae
about_quotes.evidence: We called Van to put it down on paper for her and Van made the arrangements. I had hired Van

about_quotes.speaker: John LaBarbera
about_quotes.evidence: Spud Murphy, Johnny Warrington, all those guys could write these things, Van Alexander.
